posts.text: Ahh, so it's THIS vessel. This is ARISTA (9348493). She is Comoros-flagged (as far as being "foreign" goes) but is part of Iran's Shamkhani network. ARISTA has been under US OFAC sanctions since last summer. She's actually been stuck in this same spot since mid-March 2026; at https://t.co/tqqKnos5mt
posts.summary: A sanctioned Iranian tanker has been immobilized in the same location since mid-March 2026, potentially indicating disrupted oil shipments from Iran's network. The vessel's prolonged inactivity could have implications for global oil supply if it represents a broader pattern of sanctions enforcement affecting Iranian crude exports.

posts.text:
🚨🚨🚨 We have the first Persian Gulf country lifting its oil exports to (if not even above) pre-war levels: UAE.

Sure, the 'ketchup effect' may be inflating exports a bit (from inventories). But whatever that contribution is, clearly June oil exports have surged to >95% of
posts.summary: The UAE has increased its oil exports to or above pre-war levels in June, suggesting a significant recovery in Persian Gulf production, though some of this surge may be attributed to inventory drawdowns rather than purely increased output capacity.

posts.text: It'll be interesting to learn how a vessel with an AIS-reported draft depth of 10 meters ran aground in 35-50 meters of water. https://t.co/eAlKhO0I82
posts.summary: A vessel with a reported 10-meter draft ran aground in waters 35-50 meters deep, raising questions about the accuracy of AIS data used to track ship movements. The incident highlights potential reliability issues in maritime monitoring systems that oil market participants depend on for supply chain visibility.

posts.text: and this is partially why you're seeing oil trade lower today, but gasoline is higher... https://t.co/epJu2t8J6y https://t.co/s3tiCKsnIQ
posts.summary: Patrick De Haan notes a divergence in energy markets where oil prices are declining while gasoline prices are rising, attributing this partial disconnect to factors referenced in attached links.

posts.text: this impacts the relationship between oil and gasoline in a notable way- LESS oil going into Russian refineries puts downward pressure on oil prices while it also means LESS gasoline, diesel, jet fuel etc globally putting upward pressure on crack spreads and prod prices https://t.co/xhifuPUCnQ
posts.summary: Reduced oil flows into Russian refineries create opposing market pressures: downward pressure on crude oil prices due to less oil demand, while simultaneously driving up gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel prices globally due to constrained refinery output and wider crack spreads.

posts.text: GLOBAL DATA CENTRES consumed almost 780 billion kilowatt-hours of electricity in 2025 up from 410 billion in 2020. Consumption has increased by an average of almost 14% per year. As a result, data centre consumption was equivalent to about 2.5% of global generation up from 1.5% https://t.co/PMQthn9IHe
posts.summary: Data centre electricity consumption has nearly doubled since 2020 and continues growing at 14% annually, potentially increasing demand for fossil fuel-based power generation and supporting crude oil markets through higher energy needs. The sector's rising electricity consumption could drive incremental demand for natural gas and oil-fired power plants, particularly in regions where renewable capacity remains insufficient.

posts.text: U.S. crude inventories posted another major draw, signaling resilient demand and tighter physical oil markets. The latest EIA data adds to evidence that inventories continue to shrink despite heightened geopolitical uncertainty. #Oil #EIA #CrudeOil #EnergyMarkets
posts.summary: U.S. crude inventories are declining significantly, indicating strong demand and a tightening physical oil market despite ongoing geopolitical risks. The EIA data suggests continued inventory reduction, which could support higher oil prices if the drawdown trend persists.

posts.text: Watch the US Gulf Coast closely in the next 90 days. Gasoline stocks ~10 mil bbl below last 2 years; runs maxed out; and likely foreign demand for 1-mil b/d of gasoline and distillate. PADD 3 is the "cleanest dirty shirt" for dozens of countries that need gasoline and distillate.
posts.summary: The US Gulf Coast (PADD 3) faces tight gasoline supply conditions over the next 90 days due to inventory levels running 10 million barrels below historical averages and maxed-out refinery capacity, while strong international demand for gasoline and distillate positions the region as a critical export hub for global markets.

posts.text:
U.S. Oil Recovery Threatened by Lower Prices

U.S. oil production accelerated to a record rate after the war with Iran and closure of the Strait of Hormuz boosted prices and dispelled some of the gloom hanging over the industry at the end of 2025.

Total production of crude and https://t.co/YnbgrLhSCN
posts.summary: U.S. oil production reached record levels following geopolitical tensions involving Iran and the Strait of Hormuz, which elevated crude prices and improved industry sentiment after a difficult end to 2025. However, the post indicates this production surge may now be at risk due to declining oil prices.

posts.text: Venezuela's oil exports fell slightly to 1.2 million barrels per day last month from 1.24 million bpd in May as two deadly earthquakes caused minor delays at terminals operated by state-run energy company PDVSA. https://t.co/uUMmEz4pLG
posts.summary: Venezuela's oil exports declined slightly to 1.2 million barrels per day in June from 1.24 million bpd in May, with recent earthquakes causing minor disruptions at PDVSA terminals. The modest decrease reflects temporary operational delays rather than structural damage to the country's oil production infrastructure.

posts.text:
Weekly arbs update: Europe remains long prompt, with North Sea barrels under pressure and Atlantic Basin flows seeing limited pull. That leaves DFLs carrying more of the adjustment.

What to watch: SoH transits could further disruption slow westbound Gulf flows and put a floor https://t.co/EIMbSXLQ9v
posts.summary: Europe's crude oil market shows structural oversupply in prompt barrels with North Sea production pressuring prices, while reduced Atlantic Basin exports are forcing Distillate Fuel Oil (DFO) markets to absorb more of the supply adjustment. Potential disruptions to Strait of Hormuz transits could further constrain westbound flows from the Gulf, creating price support.

posts.text: Russia is importing gasoline from India after Ukrainian drone strikes knocked out an estimated 30% of its refining capacity. Moscow plans to import 400,000 tons per month as it scrambles to ease worsening domestic fuel shortages. #Russia #Oil #Gasoline #Ukraine
posts.summary: Russia is turning to Indian gasoline imports to compensate for a 30% reduction in domestic refining capacity caused by Ukrainian drone strikes, with plans to import 400,000 tons monthly to address fuel shortages. This shift reflects tightening supply constraints in Russia's energy sector and increased reliance on alternative import sources.

posts.text:
Lots of attention on refining 3-2-1 crack spreads vs. recent oil price movements (for good reason).

However, there are other things behind refined product tightness this go around.

RVO compliance costs, included in wholesale RBOB (gasoline blendstock) and ULSD is above $15/bbl https://t.co/D09wRVkjY4 https://t.co/sJp8Diyj9N
posts.summary: Renewable fuel compliance costs embedded in gasoline and diesel prices have risen above $15 per barrel, contributing to refined product tightness alongside traditional crack spread dynamics. This suggests that regulatory mandates, rather than just crude oil pricing, are meaningfully impacting refining margins and fuel costs.

posts.text: Iran’s Araghchi warns of “powerful response” after Israel says supreme leader “marked for death” https://t.co/EtKFcw7QUe
posts.summary: Iran's foreign minister has warned of a "powerful response" following Israeli statements about targeting Iran's supreme leader, raising tensions that could disrupt Middle Eastern stability and oil supplies. Such escalation typically pressures crude prices upward due to geopolitical risk concerns in a key oil-producing region.

posts.text: EIA: Mostly neutral report - - as predicted, gasoline demand recovers but refiners run at high rate of 17.4-mbd and push nearly 10-mbd of motor fuel to market. Gulf Coast deserves scrutiny- stocks of mogas down nearly 10-mil bbl from late June 2024 and 2025. Could be worrisome.
posts.summary: U.S. refiners are operating at high capacity (17.4 million barrels daily) and pushing substantial motor fuel volumes to market as gasoline demand recovers, though Gulf Coast gasoline inventories have declined significantly since mid-2024, which could present supply concerns. The report is characterized as mostly neutral on the broader market outlook.

Yet refinery runs can't keep pace. With forward margins well above seasonal norms, even if refineries run at maximum throughput it will take until https://t.co/U2dnMXzOry

posts.text:
Carbon capture faces political opposition, project delays and high costs, but demand isn't disappearing.

For hard to abate industries, CCUS remains one of the few viable decarbonisation options. @WoodMackenzie expects strong long term growth despite today's challenges.

Read
posts.summary: Carbon capture and utilization/storage (CCUS) faces near-term headwinds from political opposition, delays, and costs, but remains essential for decarbonizing hard-to-abate industries and is expected to grow significantly long-term. The technology's importance for industrial decarbonization suggests sustained investment and development despite current challenges.

The industry shift isn’t about more data sitting in more dashboards. It’s about data that moves with your decisions, at the pace of your decisions. 

CERA Titan is about seeing the signals, not the noise, in https://t.co/o4LgyQzQ6I

posts.text: Refiner Phillips 66, which earlier this year agreed to a contract to buy directly from PDVSA, received at least two cargoes bound for the US, while trading firm Novum Energy is loading a cargo for July. At the Orinoco Belt, the Petromonagas project restarted a delayed coker.
posts.summary: Phillips 66 and Novum Energy have received or are loading Venezuelan crude oil shipments despite US sanctions, while the Petromonagas project has resumed delayed coker operations at the Orinoco Belt. These developments indicate continued Venezuelan oil supply reaching the US market and renewed production capacity in Venezuela's heavy crude sector.
